note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split only at the script's <break> tags and sentence/comma boundaries into caption-sized pieces; no word is rewritten, summarized, or reordered. Block windows were measured by normalized FFT cross-correlation (numpy rfft) of each WC-15-01-sNN.mp3 leading segment against the joined master decoded to 8 kHz mono PCM: the Sigma(ffprobe audio+0.4) formula predicts 133.220s but the ffprobed master is 132.911s (0.309s off, just past the +/-0.3s gate), so measured onsets are used instead. Every block matched at corr=1.000 and every onset lands exactly on the 0.08s mp3-frame grid; the per-file ffprobe durations are inflated ~0.03s each by mp3 encoder-delay padding that the PCM concat strips, and the error accumulates. Each block startS is its measured onset; final end = ffprobed master duration 132.911s. This is a doc-sourced (title-card / document-over-narration) lesson: every phrase is a Migration FAQ fact, carried by the captions.

phrases.text: Migration questions come with a client's system on the line, so the answers have to be exact.
Everything in this lesson comes straight from the Migration FAQ.
If a question falls outside it, you park it and ask.
You never improvise.
Here is the whole client-side story.
To reach WashCentral, they need one thing: the new URL.
It comes to them directly, or WashAssist redirects them automatically.
That is it.
This next part prevents most migration support calls.
Credentials do not change.
Users are not re-created, and data is not copied over.
WashCentral runs on the WashAssist data.
So a migrated client signs in with the exact login they already had.
For brand-new clients, nothing about your process changes.
The Pre-Configuration team onboards them the same way as always.
The only difference is the URL they are given.
Switching an existing client depends on the phase.
During beta, you submit a Jira ticket on the CSI board, and Development redirects the URL.
Once it reaches general availability,
Tech Support makes the switch directly in the Admin Portal, no ticket needed.
One consequence to know.
After the redirect, the client can no longer reach WashAssist.
The only way back is to reverse the switch.
And it does reverse.
The same path that made the switch undoes it.
In beta, that is another Jira ticket on the CSI board to revert the redirection.
So when a client asks to move back to WashAssist for month-end, that is your answer.
Clients often ask what becomes of the old system.
WashAssist goes into support mode, critical fixes only,
for a period still to be set, and is eventually retired.
Their data stays available in WashCentral the whole time,
because WashCentral was already using it.
So the short version you can say with confidence:
the login stays the same, only the URL changes,
the switch is a CSI Jira ticket in beta or an Admin Portal action at general availability,
it is fully reversible, and no data is lost.
Anything the FAQ does not answer, you park and confirm.
